page: Microsoft Azure FX64-32ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes 260 MB of L3 cache and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. The server is equipped with 1344.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong memory and processing capabilities. The server achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core performance, image processing, and text processing. Memory bandwidth is also notable, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 239.18 GB/sec and write speeds at 136.4 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is supported by a Passmark CPU score of 52418.89 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 18505.0. However, while small and medium LLM prompt processing speeds are strong, large-model text generation is weak at 2.64 tokens/sec, highlighting a performance tradeoff for massive model inference without dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX64-32ms_v2 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that require rapid access to large datasets without the overhead of massive core counts. The combination of a high 4.2 GHz clock speed and substantial RAM makes this server appropriate for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-bound scientific simulations, heavy text and image processing pipelines, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32ms_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the FXmsv2 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 16 physical cores, 32 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz, and a massive 1344.0 GB of RAM, providing 42.0 GB of memory per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but is premium storage capable. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core, image, and text processing performance, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth of 239.18 GB/sec. While multi-core and cryptography performance are strong, large-scale LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck due to the lack of hardware acceleration. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, such as large in-memory databases, heavy data processing, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz, and 1344 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, and 1344 GB of RAM for memory-intensive tasks.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense virtual server featuring 1344 GB of RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
1344.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing benchmark scores
Strong large-block memory read bandwidth of 239.18 GB/sec
No local storage or GPU, requiring external premium storage
Best suited for in-memory databases and memory-bound data processing
